response: India has approved a $1.1 billion state-backed venture capital fund focused on funding startups in areas like AI and advanced manufacturing. This builds on previous efforts but aims to be more targeted, focusing on deep-tech and manufacturing startups with longer time horizons and larger investments. The program follows recent changes easing regulatory burdens for these sectors. India is also set to host the India AI Impact Summit, bringing global tech companies alongside Indian corporates. Despite challenges in securing private capital for startups, India's startup ecosystem has grown significantly since 2016.
